Equivalent patterns conflict at wire time instead of panicking at startup.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

//fabrik:http GET /users/{id}
func ByID(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http GET /users/{name}
func ByName(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http GET /a/{x}/c
func X(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http GET /a/b/{y}
func Y(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/diags --
error: web/web.go:8:1: route GET /users/{name} conflicts with GET /users/{id}
  help: declared at $WORK/web/web.go:5:1; the patterns match the same requests and neither is more specific
error: web/web.go:14:1: route GET /a/b/{y} conflicts with GET /a/{x}/c
  help: declared at $WORK/web/web.go:11:1; the patterns match the same requests and neither is more specific
